name: backlink-pilot
description: Automated backlink submission toolkit for indie hackers

data_files.config.yaml: user product config (gitignored, copy from config.example.yaml)
data_files.targets.yaml: directory of ~250 sites with auto/status flags and auto_blocked_reason

exit_codes.1: user-actionable failure — surface nextSteps to user, do not auto-retry

json_output: Pass --json to any command that supports it. JSON-mode failures still exit 1 but write a structured payload to stdout: { status, code, error, nextSteps[], verdict }
engine: bb (bb-browser); playwright was removed in v2.2

commands.name: doctor
commands.purpose: Verify environment health (Node version, bb-browser, Chrome, config). Run this first if anything misbehaves.

commands.name: submit
commands.purpose: Submit current product to a single directory site. Accepts a known adapter name (e.g. 'futuretools') or a URL (uses generic adapter). Failures auto-write verdict to targets.yaml unless --no-auto-verdict.

commands.name: batch-submit
commands.syntax: node src/cli.js batch-submit [--limit N] [--dry-run] [--yes] [--category KEY] [--priority high|medium|low] [--force site1,site2]
commands.purpose: Run submission across many targets with dedup. Already-submitted (site, productHash) pairs are skipped automatically. Failures populate the verdict layer so the auto:yes pool self-corrects.

commands.name: status
commands.purpose: Show submission history aggregated by status. Cheap; safe to call anytime.

commands.name: locked
commands.purpose: List sites the verdict layer has auto-blocked, grouped by code. Codes: PAGE_404 / IFRAME_FORM / NO_FIELDS / LOGIN_REQUIRED / PAID_WALL / UNKNOWN_ERROR. Use --code to drill into one bucket.

commands.name: unlock
commands.purpose: Reverse a verdict lock — auto:yes restored, auto_blocked_reason dropped. Use after fixing the underlying issue (new adapter, updated submit_url) so the next batch run retries the site.

commands.name: scout
commands.purpose: Diagnose a single site's submit page — discover form fields and submit buttons. Use before writing a new adapter or to check if a NO_FIELDS site is fixable.

commands.name: triage
commands.syntax: node src/cli.js triage [--browser] [--limit N] [--category KEY] [--json] [--output FILE]
commands.purpose: Classify all auto:yes targets into buckets (generic-ready / adapter-needed / iframe-provider / manual-review) before running batch-submit. HTTP-only by default; pass --browser for full snapshot triage.

verdict_codes.PAID_WALL: Page is paywalled or submissions closed → status:paid, auto:no
verdict_codes.IFRAME_FORM: Form is in a Typeform/Tally/Airtable iframe → auto:no (immediate)
verdict_codes.NO_FIELDS: No recognizable form fields after long-wait scan → auto:no (after 2 consecutive failures)
verdict_codes.CHROME_TIMEOUT: Chrome unresponsive — transient, no targets.yaml change
verdict_codes.CAPTCHA_FAILED: Captcha unsolved — may have submitted, no targets.yaml change
